sections.title: Leadership
sections.summary: CEO Dara Khosrowshahi operates under a June 2023 employment agreement, leading a team that delivered $37.3 billion in FY2025 revenue and a fifth consecutive year of 20%+ constant-currency Gross Bookings growth. COO Andrew Macdonald oversees global operations across 70 countries, while the CFO role transitions from Prashanth Mahendra-Rajah to Balaji Krishnamurthy.
points: Dara Khosrowshahi serves as CEO and Director under an employment agreement dated June 28, 2023, overseeing a platform with 9.7 million Drivers and Couriers and over 40 million daily trips.
Andrew Macdonald serves as President and COO, managing operations across three segments-Mobility, Delivery, and Freight-and four geographic regions spanning 70-plus countries.
The CFO position is transitioning from Prashanth Mahendra-Rajah, who signed the FY2025 10-K, to incoming Balaji Krishnamurthy as noted in the 2026 proxy statement.

sections.title: Capital Allocation
sections.summary: Uber generated $9.8 billion in FY2025 free cash flow and deployed capital across growing share repurchases, AV technology investments, and acquisitions including the $694 million Trendyol GO deal. The company also increased its Freight subsidiary ownership to 90% while maintaining $4.7 billion in cash against $8.3 billion in long-term debt.
points: During 2025, Uber deployed capital into growing share repurchases and strategic autonomous vehicle investments, supported by $9.76 billion in free cash flow and $3.59 billion in operating cash flow.
Uber completed an $694 million all-cash acquisition of an 85% stake in Trendyol GO in June 2025, assigning $712 million in goodwill to the Delivery segment.
Uber increased its Freight Holding ownership to 90% of capital stock (85% fully-diluted) as of December 31, 2025, up from 84% (80% fully-diluted) a year earlier.
The balance sheet shows $4.68 billion in cash and equivalents against $8.35 billion in long-term debt, with senior notes maturing from 2027 to 2054 and a revolving credit facility available.

sections.title: Shareholder Alignment
sections.summary: Uber's compensation structure ties executive pay to performance metrics including 20%+ annual Gross Bookings growth, Adjusted EBITDA expansion from $6.5 billion to $8.7 billion, and free cash flow generation. The company's September 2025 addition to the S&P 100 reflects sustained shareholder value creation across five consecutive growth years.
points: Uber achieved its fifth consecutive year of 20%+ annual Gross Bookings growth on a constant-currency basis in 2025, supporting the performance-linked compensation framework referenced in the proxy.
Adjusted EBITDA grew from $6,484 million in FY2024 to $8,730 million in FY2025, providing a measurable performance benchmark for executive incentive alignment.
Uber joined the S&P 100 in September 2025, reflecting sustained market capitalization growth and shareholder value creation under the current leadership team.

sections.title: Communication
sections.summary: Uber reports financial results across three operating segments-Mobility, Delivery, and Freight-and four geographic regions, with detailed disclosures including insurance reserves, AV partner counts, and membership metrics. The 10-K and DEF 14A provide granular data on cross-platform engagement, advertising revenue, and acquisition goodwill allocation.
points: Uber's 10-K discloses three operating segments-Mobility, Delivery, and Freight-alongside four geographic regions: United States and Canada, Latin America, EMEA, and Asia Pacific.

The DEF 14A provides specific operating metrics including 46 million Uber One members (up 55% year-over-year), over $2 billion in annualized advertising revenue, and 25-plus AV partners across seven live cities.
The 10-K details insurance reserves of $12,463 million as of December 31, 2025, up from $9,796 million in 2024, with $473 million covered by third-party insurance.
Cross-platform engagement metrics are disclosed: 20% of customers use both Mobility and Delivery monthly, and multi-product users show 35% higher retention and 3x spending.

sections.title: Governance
sections.summary: Independent Chairperson Ronald Sugar leads a board of ten directors including Ursula Burns, Nikesh Arora, and Revathi Advaithi, with David Trujillo scheduled to depart after the 2026 Annual Meeting. The board oversees risk factors including $12.5 billion in insurance reserves and a pending $435 million Getir acquisition in Türkiye.
points: The Board is chaired by Independent Chairperson Ronald Sugar and includes directors Revathi Advaithi, Turqi Alnowaiser, Nikesh Arora, Ursula Burns, Robert Eckert, Amanda Ginsberg, Dara Khosrowshahi, John Thain, David Trujillo, and Alexander Wynaendts.
David Trujillo will transition off the Board following the 2026 Annual Meeting, reducing board size while maintaining independent oversight under Chairperson Sugar.
The 10-K discloses insurance reserves of $12.46 billion as a material balance sheet risk item, growing 27% year-over-year from $9.80 billion.

A pending $435 million cash acquisition of Getir's food delivery business in Türkiye, announced February 8, 2026, represents an active governance oversight item with expected closing in the second half of 2026.

types.summary: Uber's platform connects 9.7 million Drivers and Couriers with riders and eaters across 70-plus countries, generating over 40 million daily trips. Cross-platform engagement reinforces this: 40% of customers use multiple products monthly, and multi-product users show 35% higher retention and 3x spending versus single-product users.

Uber One's 46 million members across 47 countries create recurring engagement through subscription benefits, growing 55% year-over-year in 2025. Members who engage across Mobility and Delivery demonstrate materially higher retention and spending, increasing the cost of switching to competing platforms.

With $37.3 billion in FY2025 revenue and operations spanning over 70 countries, Uber achieves density advantages that enable $8.7 billion in Adjusted EBITDA. The platform's scale supports an advertising business exceeding $2 billion in annualized revenue, growing 50% year-over-year through Sponsored Listings and Sponsored Items.

Uber's technology platform and data assets, supported by $3.16 billion in FY2025 R&D spending, underpin relationships with over 1.3 million monthly Merchants and 25-plus autonomous vehicle partners. AV deployments in seven cities globally demonstrate proprietary network integration capabilities that competitors cannot easily replicate.
